answer:
The Stripe API primarily uses HTTP Basic Auth to authenticate requests, where you provide your secret API key as the username without a password [1][2][3]. For cross-origin requests, you should use Bearer authentication instead [1][3]. All requests must be made over HTTPS [1][4][2].

For cURL requests, use the following format [1][5][3]:

```sh
curl https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges \
  -u sk_test_your_key_here:
```

Alternatively, for Bearer authentication, use the header [1][6][3]:

```sh
curl https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges \
  -H "Authorization: REDACTED_EXAMPLE"
```

For Stripe Apps, developers can also configure authentication using Platform keys, OAuth 2.0, or Restricted API keys by modifying the app manifest [7].
